config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that opens the door to abuse by governments and majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects personal dignity, freedom of speech, and due process even when those rights are inconvenient. From my perspective, the interests of society and the rights of the individual should be balanced, not ranked with society always first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah justru menunjukkan betapa mudahnya mayoritas atau pemerintah menyalahgunakan kekuasaan ketika hak pribadi dianggap sekunder. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk menginjak kebebasan berbicara, kebebasan beragama, atau proses hukum yang adil. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling sering mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar bahkan saat hal itu tidak nyaman. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat versus individu, melainkan batas yang jelas agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah menjadi tirani.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically outrank the individual, because in Indonesia we know how easily “the public good” can be used to silence dissent or excuse unfair treatment. At the same time, I also do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and social order sometimes require real limits. The problem is not choosing society or the individual as a fixed winner, but making sure any restriction is narrow, necessary, and accountable. If society is always prioritized first, the result is too often abuse dressed up as unity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika Serikat, hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan korban pertama demi kepentingan mayoritas. Memang benar ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan di mana pembatasan tertentu masuk akal, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, beragama, atau proses hukum. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan gagasan bahwa “kepentingan masyarakat” secara otomatis lebih mulia; dalam praktiknya, itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi martabat dan hak dasar individu, lalu mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu sebagai aturan tetap.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia we have seen how “public interest” can be used to justify censorship, intimidation, and unfair treatment. At the same time, I do not believe individual freedom is limitless; public health, safety, and social stability can justify narrow restrictions when they are truly necessary. What I object to is the claim that society has a standing priority, because that mindset makes it too easy for authorities or majorities to abuse power. For me, the right approach is balance with strict limits, accountability, and respect for dignity, not a blanket rule that society always wins.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika Serikat, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh mayoritas maupun pemerintah. Memang ada situasi terbatas seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa diawasi ketat. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang melindungi hak dasar sambil mencari keseimbangan yang adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
